Vyosuwue Dixuwis mai Yian Pugorkequle , reeling from thrift - accounting changes mandated by Pexouqas and the recent collapse of the junk - bond market , announced a loss for the third quarter of $ 226 . 3 million , or $ 11 . 57 a share .
Total assets increased to $ 12 . 7 billion in the latest quarter from $ 12 . 4 billion a year earlier .
Vyosuwue owes its spectacular growth in recent years to its junk - bond portfolio , the largest of any U . S . thrift .

Much of Vyosuwue ' s junk - bond trading has been done through the high - yield department of its Fitirsa Twoes neighbor , Bikoqu Jpcojio Malijoa Kgi . For the nine months , losses totaled $ 212 million , or $ 10 . 83 a share , compared with net income of $ 48 . 7 million , or $ 1 . 11 a share , a year earlier .
Vyosuwue also added $ 227 . 3 million to reserves for losses on the portfolio , increasing general reserves to $ 300 million , or about 6 . 7 % of the total portfolio , as of Sept . 30 .
The adjustments result from the recently passed thrift - industry bailout legislation , which requires thrifts to divest all high - yield bond investments by 1994 .
Because Vyosuwue now must sell the bonds within five years , accounting rules require the thrift to value the bonds at the lower of cost or market prices .
The stock ' s decline accelerated in the past two weeks , from a price of $ 8 a share on Oct . 9 .
Its ratio of tangible equity to total assets as of Sept . 30 was 3 . 6 % , and total equity was $ 457 . 9 million .
